topics.name: 100 Level: Pre-Medical Foundation Sciences
topics.description: General Biology, General Chemistry, General Physics, and Medical Mathematics foundation courses.

subtopics.name: BIO 101 & CHE 101: Cell Biology and Organic Chemistry
objectives: Understand cell ultrastructure, biomolecules, genetics, and metabolic pathways
Study functional groups, stereochemistry, and organic reactions relevant to biological systems

subtopics.name: PHY 101: Physics for Medical Sciences
objectives: Apply fluid mechanics, optics, wave motion, radiation physics, and thermodynamics to human physiology

topics.name: 200 Level: Basic Medical Sciences (1st Professional MBBS Part 1)
topics.description: Gross Anatomy, Embryology, Histology, Medical Biochemistry, and Human Physiology.

subtopics.name: ANAT 201: Gross Anatomy, Histology, and Embryology
objectives: Dissect and identify anatomical structures of upper limbs, lower limbs, thorax, abdomen, pelvis, head, and neck
Identify microscopic tissue architecture under light microscopy (Histology)
Trace human embryonic development from fertilization to organogenesis (Embryology)

subtopics.name: BIOC 201 & PHYS 201: Medical Biochemistry and Human Physiology
objectives: Study carbohydrate, lipid, protein, and nucleic acid metabolism and enzymatic regulation
Analyze physiological mechanisms of cardiovascular, respiratory, renal, gastrointestinal, and endocrine systems

topics.name: 300 Level: Pathology and Pharmacology (2nd Professional MBBS Part 2)
topics.description: Anatomical Pathology, Haematology, Chemical Pathology, Medical Microbiology, and Pharmacology.

subtopics.name: PATH 301: General and Systemic Pathology
objectives: Understand mechanisms of cellular injury, inflammation, tissue repair, neoplasia, and hemodynamic disorders
Analyze disease etiologies and histopathological changes in major organ systems

subtopics.name: PHARM 301: Pharmacology and Therapeutics
objectives: Study pharmacokinetics (absorption, distribution, metabolism, excretion) and pharmacodynamics (drug-receptor interactions)
Analyze therapeutic actions and toxicities of autonomic, cardiovascular, antimicrobial, and CNS drugs

topics.name: 400 - 500 Level: Clinical Rotations and Final MBBS
topics.description: Obstetrics & Gynaecology, Paediatrics, Community Medicine, Internal Medicine, General Surgery, and Final Board Defense.

subtopics.name: CLIN 401: Obstetrics, Gynaecology, and Paediatrics
objectives: Manage antenatal care, labor, delivery, obstetric emergencies, and gynaecological disorders
Diagnose and treat neonatal disorders, childhood infections, malnutrition, and developmental pediatrics

subtopics.name: CLIN 501: Internal Medicine and General Surgery
objectives: Conduct clinical history taking, physical examinations, and differential diagnosis in internal medicine
Perform surgical ward rounds, emergency resuscitation, and basic operative surgical procedures
Pass Final MBBS clinical ward examinations and oral viva defense before Medical and Dental Council of Nigeria (MDCN) examiners
